claim: On June 23, 2021 — one day before the Champlain Towers South collapse in Florida — software pioneer John McAfee, 75, was found dead in his cell at the Brians 2 prison near Barcelona, Spain, hours after the Spanish National Court approved his extradition to the United States on federal tax-evasion charges. Barcelona's court later ruled the death a suicide by hanging following a full investigation that included prison footage and an autopsy. McAfee's widow Janice publicly disputed the suicide ruling. McAfee himself had posted, in a 2019 tweet, the statement “If I suicide myself, I didn't. I was whackd.” In a separate June 2019 tweet from Cuba, McAfee threatened to release “31+ terabytes of incriminating data” on government corruption if he were arrested or disappeared — the “dead man's switch” claim that has anchored online speculation about his death ever since. In the days after his death and the next-day Surfside collapse, a screenshot of a purported June 8, 2021 McAfee tweet circulated widely, reading: “If anything ever happens to me, please know that the 31TB of files I have are located on hard drives in my condo near 88th Street and Collins Avenue just north of Miami Beach” — the address of Champlain Towers South. No archive snapshot of @officialmcafee that postdates the alleged June 8 tweet shows it: the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine captured McAfee's Twitter timeline on June 20, 2021 — twelve days after the alleged tweet — and it does not appear, nor does it appear in the multiple capture-day snapshots from June 23-24. The Wayback's capture density during the relevant window is a June 2 snapshot followed by an 18-day gap until June 20, so the alleged tweet could only have existed if it was posted and then deleted within that gap with no contemporaneous archive capture. No contemporaneous quote-tweet, reply, or news mention of it from June 8-22 has surfaced; every reference postdates his June 23 death and the June 24 collapse. PolitiFact, Snopes, BOOM Live, and Misbar each independently checked his account history and concluded the screenshot was not authentic. Miami-Dade property records show McAfee did not own a unit at Champlain Towers. The 2019 dead-man's-switch tweet is on the record; the June 2021 Champlain Towers tweet is not.

sources.label: Wikipedia — “John McAfee”
sources.quote: On 23 June 2021, McAfee was found dead in his prison cell, hours after the Spanish National Court ordered his extradition to the United States on criminal charges.

sources.label: NPR — “John McAfee, Software Pioneer, Found Dead In A Spanish Prison Cell” (Jun 23, 2021)
CNBC — “John McAfee dead of apparent suicide in Spanish jail after court approves his extradition to U.S. on tax charges” (Jun 23, 2021)
Anadolu Agency — “Barcelona court rules John McAfee's death in prison was suicide”
PolitiFact — “Image suggesting John McAfee stored files at collapsed Fla. condo tower is fake” (June 28, 2021).
sources.quote: McAfee sent out two tweets on June 8, but neither made reference to computer files or a condo

sources.label: Snopes — “Did John McAfee Hide Files at Collapsed Miami Building?” (June 28, 2021).
Misbar — “John McAfee Didn't Own a Condo in Collapsed Florida Building” (July 3, 2021).

Internet Archive Wayback Machine — snapshot of twitter.com/officialmcafee taken June 20, 2021 at 10:24 UTC, twelve days after the alleged June 8 tweet. The alleged tweet does not appear in the captured timeline.
BOOM Live — “John McAfee's Doctored Tweet Misleadingly Linked To Miami Condo Collapse” (July 2, 2021), independent fact-check that used the same June 20, 2021 Wayback snapshot as a primary source.
Fight Back Podcast / @jakeshieldspod, Instagram reel DRoJ9FFiSUv (Nov 28, 2025) — surfacing source

notes: Anchor y2021-surfside. The 24-hour gap between McAfee's death (June 23) and the Champlain Towers South collapse (June 24) is the timing that animates the connection narrative. The 2019 dead-man's-switch tweet and the “If I suicide myself” tweet are both on McAfee's verified Twitter account. The June 2021 Champlain Towers tweet circulating in viral screenshots is not supported by any contemporaneous record: the June 20, 2021 Wayback snapshot of @officialmcafee does not contain it; no quote-tweet, reply, or news mention from the June 8-22 window references it; and Miami-Dade property records show no McAfee ownership at Champlain Towers. The honest evidentiary caveat is that the Wayback density during the relevant window has an 18-day gap (June 2 to June 20), so the alleged tweet could only have existed if posted and deleted entirely within that gap with no archive or downstream propagation surviving — a possibility that is not zero but is also not supported by any positive evidence. Both halves are kept in the claim so the reader can see what part of the link is on the record (the timing, the 2019 31TB tweet) and what part is not (the specific Champlain Towers tweet, the unit ownership).
